satire_attempt: Senior engineer at Stripe-clone-#47 explains to junior that 'the merchant's chargeback need predates the founders' as the team architects a fourth ledger rewrite this decade. PM nods sagely while the company burns through Series C. The Visa rulebook predates everyone in this room, including the WeWork lease. Build the forty-year platform, in the next eighteen months, before runway runs out. Now back to standup.
verdict_reason: The satire works only by stripping out the verse's actual specificity (custodianship under team turnover, the falsifiability ruling out 'eternal requirement = ossify implementation', the counter-example carving out novel greenfield work). The verse's claim — that under-architecting via tenure-scoped reasoning is a real failure mode and the corrective is a custodianship frame — is too specific to mock without disagreeing with it. The satire requires inventing a 'fourth ledger rewrite this decade' which the verse explicitly warns against in the falsifiability section. PASS.
